words.def: a place where children go to be educated
words.example: My brother and I went to the same school.

words.def: knowledge about the structure and behaviour of the natural and physical world, based on facts that you can prove, for example by experiments
words.example: new developments in science and technology

words.def: a person who studies one or more of the natural sciences (= for example, physics, chemistry or biology)
words.example: a research scientist

words.def: the salt water that covers most of the earth’s surface and surrounds its continents and islands
words.example: The waste was dumped in the sea.

words.def: any of the parts into which something is divided
words.example: section of something, That section of the road is still closed.

words.def: to become aware of somebody/something by using your eyes
words.example: see somebody/something, She looked for him but couldn't see him in the crowd.

words.def: to give something to somebody in exchange for money
words.example: sell (something), I recently sold my bike.

words.def: the 9th month of the year, between August and October
words.example: She was born in September.
There are seven cookies left.

words.def: a female person or animal that has already been mentioned or is easily identified
words.example: ‘What does your sister do?’ ‘She's a dentist.’

words.def: an animal with a thick coat, kept on farms for its meat (called mutton or lamb) or its wool
words.example: a flock of sheep

words.def: a piece of clothing worn on the upper part of the body, made of light cloth, with sleeves and usually with a collar and buttons down the front
words.example: to wear a shirt

words.def: one of a pair of objects usually made of leather or plastic that you wear on your feet
words.example: a pair of shoes

words.def: a building or part of a building where you can buy goods or services
words.example: to open/close/run a shop

words.def: the activity of going to shops and buying things or ordering them online
words.example: to go shopping

words.def: measuring or covering a small length or distance, or a smaller length or distance than usual
words.example: He had short curly hair.

words.def: used to show what is right, appropriate, etc., especially when criticizing somebody’s actions
words.example: You shouldn't drink and drive.

words.def: a piece of equipment producing a flow of water that you stand under to wash yourself; the small room or part of a room that contains a shower
words.example: in the shower, He's in the shower.

words.def: physically or mentally ill
words.example: a sick child

words.def: to make musical sounds with your voice in the form of a song or tune
words.example: I just can't sing in tune!

words.def: a person who sings, or whose job is singing, especially in public
words.example: She's a wonderful singer.

words.def: a girl or woman who has the same mother and father as another person
words.example: She's my sister.

words.def: to rest your weight on your bottom with your back straight, for example on/in a chair
words.example: She sat and stared at the letter in front of her.
There are six cookies left.

words.def: the ability to do something well
words.example: The job requires skill and an eye for detail.

words.def: a piece of clothing that hangs from the middle part of the body
words.example: a short/long/pleated/denim skirt

words.def: not large in size, number, degree, amount, etc.
words.example: a small town/village/community

words.def: a reptile with a very long thin body and no legs. There are many types of snake, some of which are poisonous.
words.example: a snake coiled up in the grass

words.def: small soft white pieces (called flakes) of frozen water that fall from the sky in cold weather; this substance when it is lying on the ground
words.example: Snow was falling heavily.

words.def: used with uncountable nouns or plural countable nouns to mean ‘an amount of’ or ‘a number of’, when the amount or number is not given
words.example: There's some milk in the fridge.

words.def: a person who is not known or mentioned by name
words.example: There's someone at the door.

words.def: a thing that is not known or mentioned by name
words.example: We stopped for something to eat.

words.def: a person’s male child
words.example: their four-year-old son

words.def: a short piece of music with words that you sing
words.example: a love/pop/rock song

words.def: in a short time from now; a short time after something else has happened
words.example: She sold the house soon after her husband died.

words.def: feeling sad and ashamed about something that has been done
words.example: sorry about something, We're very sorry about the damage to your car.

words.def: a liquid food made by boiling meat, vegetables, etc. in water, often eaten as the first course of a meal
words.example: a bowl/cup of soup

words.def: to talk to somebody about something; to have a conversation with somebody
words.example: speak to somebody, The President refused to speak to reporters.

words.def: not ordinary or usual; different from what is normal
words.example: The school will only allow this in special circumstances.

words.def: to give money to pay for goods, services, etc.
words.example: spend something, I've spent all my money already.

words.def: activity that you do for pleasure and that needs physical effort or skill, usually done in a special area and according to fixed rules
words.example: There are excellent facilities for sport and recreation.

words.def: a place where trains stop so that passengers can get on and off; the buildings connected with this
words.example: the main station

words.def: a place where a bus or train stops regularly for passengers to get on or off
words.example: I get off at the next stop.

words.def: a description of events and people that the writer or speaker has invented in order to entertain people
words.example: a tragic love story

words.def: a public road in a city or town that has houses and buildings on one side or both sides
words.example: along/down/up the street, I was just walking along the street when it happened.

words.def: having a lot of physical power so that you can lift heavy weights, do hard physical work, etc.
words.example: He's strong enough to lift a car!

words.def: a person who is studying at a university or college
words.example: a college/university student

words.def: a sweet substance, often in the form of white or brown crystals, made from the juices of various plants, used in cooking or to make tea, coffee, etc. sweeter
words.example: Do you take sugar (= have it in your tea, coffee, etc.)?

words.def: the warmest season of the year, coming between spring and autumn
words.example: in the summer, We're going away in the summer.

words.def: the star that shines in the sky during the day and gives the earth heat and light
words.example: The sun was shining and birds were singing.

words.def: the day of the week after Saturday and before Monday, thought of as either the first or the last day of the week
words.example: It's Sunday today, isn't it?

words.def: a large shop that sells food, drinks and goods used in the home. People choose what they want from the shelves and pay for it as they leave.
words.example: I only shop at this supermarket.

words.def: the sport or activity of swimming
words.example: Swimming is a good form of exercise.

words.def: an informal shirt, usually with short sleeves (= arms) no collar or buttons, or just a few buttons at the top
words.example: He was wearing a faded pair of blue jeans and an old T-shirt.

words.def: a piece of furniture that consists of a flat top supported by legs
words.example: at a/the table, We sat at a round table in the corner.

words.def: having a greater than average height
words.example: She's tall and thin.

words.def: a car with a driver that you pay to take you somewhere. Taxis usually have meters that show how much money you have to pay.
words.example: We took a taxi to the airport.

words.def: the dried leaves (called tea leaves) of the tea bush
words.example: a packet of tea

words.def: to give lessons to students in a school, college, university, etc.; to help somebody learn something by giving information about it
words.example: She teaches at our local school.

words.def: a person whose job is teaching, especially in a school
words.example: an English/a science teacher

words.def: a group of people who play a particular game or sport against another group of people
words.example: a football/basketball/soccer team

words.def: a system for talking to somebody else over long distances, using wires or radio; a machine used for this
words.example: The telephone rang and Pat answered it.

words.def: a piece of electrical equipment with a screen on which you can watch programmes with moving pictures and sounds
words.example: a widescreen/flat-screen/plasma television

words.def: to give information to somebody by speaking or writing
words.example: tell something to somebody, He told the news to everybody he saw.
There are only ten of these rare animals left.

words.def: a game in which two or four players use rackets to hit a ball backwards and forwards across a net on a specially marked court
words.example: to play tennis

words.def: very unpleasant; making you feel very unhappy, upset or frightened
words.example: a terrible experience

words.def: an examination of somebody’s knowledge or ability, consisting of questions for them to answer or activities for them to perform
words.example: an IQ/a fitness test

words.def: used to introduce the second part of a comparison
words.example: I'm older than her.

words.def: to tell somebody that you are grateful for something
words.example: thank somebody for something, I must write and thank Mary for the present.

words.def: used to show that you are grateful to somebody for something they have done
words.example: ‘How are you?’ ‘Fine, thanks (= thanks for asking).’

words.def: used to refer to somebody/something that has already been mentioned or is easily understood
words.example: There were three questions. The first two were relatively easy but the third one was hard.

words.def: a building or an outdoor area where plays and similar types of entertainment are performed
words.example: Broadway theatres

words.def: of or belonging to people, animals or things that have already been mentioned or are easily identified
words.example: Their parties are always fun.

words.def: used when referring to people, animals or things as the object of a verb or preposition, or after the verb be
words.example: Tell them the news.

words.def: used to refer to a particular time in the past or future
words.example: Life was harder then because neither of us had a job.

words.def: used to show that something exists or happens
words.example: There's a restaurant around the corner.

words.def: people, animals or things that have already been mentioned or are easily identified
words.example: ‘Where are John and Liz?’ ‘They went for a walk.’

words.def: an object whose name you do not use because you do not need to or want to, or because you do not know it
words.example: Can you pass me that thing over there?

words.def: to have a particular idea or opinion about something/somebody; to believe something
words.example: think (that)…, Do you really think (that) he'll win?
There are only three cookies left.

words.def: the day of the week after Wednesday and before Friday
words.example: It's Thursday today, isn't it?

words.def: a printed piece of paper, or a message or image received on your phone or computer, that gives you the right to travel on a particular bus, train, etc. or to go into a theatre, etc.
words.example: a plane/bus/train ticket

words.def: feeling that you would like to sleep or rest; needing rest
words.example: to be/look/feel tired

words.def: used to show purpose or intention
words.example: I set out to buy food.

words.def: on this day
words.example: I've got a piano lesson later today.

words.def: with or near to somebody/something else; with each other
words.example: We grew up together.

words.def: a large bowl attached to a pipe that you sit on or stand over when you get rid of waste matter from your body
words.example: Have you flushed the toilet?

words.def: a soft fruit with a lot of juice and shiny red skin that is eaten as a vegetable either raw or cooked
words.example: a bacon, lettuce, and tomato sandwich

words.def: on or during the day after today
words.example: I'm off now. See you tomorrow.

words.def: on or during the evening or night of today
words.example: Will you have dinner with me tonight?

words.def: used before adjectives and adverbs to say that something is more than is good, necessary, possible, etc.
words.example: He's far too young to go on his own.

words.def: any of the hard white structures in the mouth used for biting food
words.example: I've just had a tooth out at the dentist's.

words.def: a subject that you talk, write or learn about
words.example: The main topic of conversation was Tom's new girlfriend.

words.def: a place with many houses, shops, etc. where people live and work. It is larger than a village but smaller than a city.
words.example: The nearest town is ten miles away.

words.def: a tall plant that can live a long time. Trees have a thick central wooden trunk from which branches grow, usually with leaves on them
words.example: an oak/olive/apple tree

words.def: a piece of clothing that covers the lower body and is divided into two parts to cover each leg separately
words.example: a pair of grey trousers

words.def: connected with facts rather than things that have been invented or guessed
words.example: Indicate whether the following statements are true or false.

words.def: the day of the week after Monday and before Wednesday
words.example: It's Tuesday today, isn't it?
